self awareness isn t spontaneous but a gradual process triggered by new experiences and shifts in perspective just as mumbaikars are slowly learning the discipline of queuing for trains a concept unheard of before the metro or parents gain new insights into societal norms through their children s experiences abroad our collective self awareness grows when we re exposed to different ways of living currently many operate from a fight for limited resources mindset seeing themselves as victims which hinders progress to truly evolve we need both enforcement of rules which provides the necessary order and fairness and empathy for the underprivileged while rules can be clearly defined fostering empathy requires a degree of sacrifice from those who are better off a willingness to understand and uplift others rather than solely focusing on one s own perceived struggles

just by the idea of it i can assure you it d be a shit show but your points 1 through 5 are completely invalid while points 6 and 7 have some logic to it on paper i get a sense that you haven t really applied for too many visas maybe specially in recent times and sorry but this entitlement that because even philippines doesn t ask you people for a visa is not a good argument to support your naivety 1 most of the visa application platforms are shit uk has a pretty good one but still far from perfect vfs global the company handling this that other people have been blasting in the comments is the same company that handles us visas as well as visas for many many other countries 2 asking your mother s name and father s name alongwith their nationality and place of birth is absolutely not ridiculous or idiotic question that s the basis of your being and all the visa applications i have filled have asked some variation of the same btw if you are an orphan you d still have some sort of legal guardianship for these matters although this is not necessarily common under extraordinary circumstances special arrangements have to be made not to compare at all this is a very bad example but i intend to point to the extremity but even your pets are supposed to have a set of documents for international travel commonly referred to as pet passport most pet owners don t have these documents but procure the same as and when needed 3 i hate what aadhar has become in india but didn t this point get a little too away from you here you want to complaint about visa process or data privacy neither india is the only country to have this sort of national id number nor were they asking you for your aadhar your passport number is applicable here in most cases or some other number depending on where you re from in us typically your driving license will suffice and i am curious what did you end up entering if not your passport number in this field that you are complaining so much this number can lead to additional screening triggers to help investigate your visa candidature it is not just a visa application that asks for such numbers btw many companies ask this as part of their kyc specially when operating internationally 4 i am an atheist here i gave up my religion like 6 yrs ago swearing i would never declare myself to be a hindu at the peak of my depression i hate that any document asks for religion but honestly if any important document like a visa application does ask for it i can t remember if any visa application has asked me this or not i choose hindu to avoid the risk of potentially being flagged as a case of perjury as an atheist in india i can tell you even though there exists a couple of judgements that make it possible for indians to officially declare themselves as atheists practically this is a pain to achieve about the form the others option is for you the purpose of the form is not to bolster your self beliefs but to screen you if someone asks religion you say atheist not no or not say anything at all there s also agnostics btw which i am yet to fully understand and there are prolly thousands other religions in the world outside of the scope of any form so be a good atheist and don t let your religion shape the conversation please 5 most countries ask for these references most employers ask for such references does it make sense no is it okay to single out india for this i don t think so this also depends on the type of visa like a typical student work visa will be satisfied with the details of your school company others not as much points 6 and 7 are valid but you know what if you apply to any other country s visa the experience will be absolutely similar i have lived in us for quite some time and lost over 50k trying to get my parents b1 b2 visa to come visit me while i was there and i never could get a visa appointment and trust me it wasn t entirely about the appointments but how they were presented tech wise same vfs global i remember thinking it s so sad that a country as great as the us cares so little about foreigners that they literally call aliens most likely just because the foreigners don t vote for the people responsible the whole visa system imo is extremely stu id and we can achieve so much more theoretically as a world by having open borders but unfortunately that s not the world we live in india has many many problems i assure you you will face so many problems while being here but this rant this is not really valid despite this long rant of my own my intention was not to hate on you but to hopefully ask you to raise voice about actual real hopeful problems

nope its not superiority complex learning languages is something that we are just really unenthusiastic about hell we wouldn t even have learnt english if we wouldn t have been forced on us by our schools and the reason for that is simple north india as in whole consist 8 states and we have naver scene language based discrimination then lets talk about maharashtra gujrat the states that speak different languages odisa west bengal there is no language based discrimination anywhere but as soon as we enter states like karnataka tamilnadu kerala andra pradesh we are bombarded with language based discrimination people will act like they can t understand but as soon as you complain about it same guy will start shouting at you in hindi and you ll realise he could understand you the whole time this was my experience as a tourist in karnataka i was in a bus talking to my friend i was asking for information about next stop from tc in bangluru and he totally acted like he couldn t understand me all i said to my friend was accha karnataka agae and suddenly tc started shouting to mat ao na phir kyu ata hai tum log yaha and not a single person in bus stood up for us so if you can understand hindi you can speak hindi then why are you so hostile towards it no one is asking anyone to stop speaking there mother tongue and no one wants to enforce hindi on you its something most of the people allready know but for us kannada is a totally alien language and learning it from scratch can take more then 7 years if we completely focus on it and in your case north indians who are also working as doctors it will be impossible for them to focus on language
